markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' decision to delay Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026, for additional polish has intensified trader focus on pre-release milestones, with pre-order windows and a potential third trailer emerging as key swing factors. Take-Two Interactive's upcoming May earnings call could provide the next official signal on marketing timelines, following leaks confirming pre-orders may open as early as May 18. Historical patterns show Rockstar typically unveils major trailers and pre-order campaigns months ahead of launch, building sustained hype through controlled reveals. Industry observers note that any confirmation of these events would likely accelerate market consensus, while further delays or quiet periods could prolong uncertainty in the run-up to the game's highly anticipated drop.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial has sharply shifted trader sentiment toward No Prison Time at 80.3 percent implied probability. A jury deadlocked 9-3 after days of testimony, prompting the judge to declare a hung jury and leaving prosecutors weighing whether to pursue a fourth trial on the same charges. Weinstein, 74, continues to serve time under his 2020 New York conviction while appealing a separate 16-year Los Angeles sentence and awaiting potential sentencing on the upheld Miriam Haley count, which could add up to 25 years. His advanced age, documented health decline, and the pattern of overturned or stalled cases have strengthened market consensus that additional prison time is unlikely to materialize before appeals or plea resolutions intervene.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes have emerged as the clear frontrunners in the 2026 Stanley Cup race due to their league-leading regular-season performances and dominant early playoff runs. The Avalanche captured the Presidents' Trophy with 121 points, boasting the NHL's top offense led by Nathan MacKinnon and Cale Makar while allowing the fewest goals against. Meanwhile, the Hurricanes secured the Eastern Conference's best record at 113 points and remain perfect through eight postseason games. This head-to-head balance, combined with Vegas Golden Knights' solid but lesser standing and the limited upside for Montreal and Buffalo, keeps implied probabilities tightly contested among traders reflecting the wisdom of the crowds on current form and roster health.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of implied probabilities among France, Spain, and England stems from their strong recent European performances and deep squads as the 2026 FIFA World Cup approaches. England follows with an unbeaten, goal-free qualification record under Thomas Tuchel, building on consistent tournament runs. These factors, combined with Brazil and Argentina’s attacking pedigrees, create a balanced field where historical form, roster health, and group-stage matchups keep multiple teams in contention for advancement through the expanded knockout stages.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2028 Democratic presidential nomination remains highly fluid with no dominant frontrunner, as traders price Gavin Newsom at 24.4 percent due to his high national profile, California governorship, and proven fundraising capacity. Kamala Harris and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follow at 8.9 and 8.6 percent, drawing residual support from prior cycles and distinct party factions, while figures like Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro sit lower amid competition from Senate and gubernatorial records. Differentiators center on swing-state appeal, progressive versus moderate policy stances, and early donor networks. Support may consolidate around stronger 2026 midterm results, primary-state polling shifts, or key endorsements once formal campaigning intensifies ahead of the Iowa caucuses.
